claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. 

I have one child - Alex, who is 20 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 20 years old, they are over the usual age limit of 16 for Child Benefit. However, you may still be eligible if Alex is in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved full-time education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or an equivalent qualification) or on an approved training course?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Based on the information you've provided, I need to let you know that Child Benefit eligibility has specific age requirements:

- A child must be **under 16**, OR
- **16, 17, 18, 19, or 20** and in approved full-time education or training

Since Alex is 20 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, I'm afraid you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with my formal assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment

Thank you for providing all the necessary information. Here is my assessment:

### Alex (Age 20)
**Eligible:** ❌ No

**Reason:** Alex is 20 years old and is not currently in approved full-time education or approved training. To qualify for Child Benefit for a child aged 16-20, the child must be enrolled in approved education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or qualifications up to and including A-level or equivalent) or on an approved training course. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

---

**Summary:** Unfortunately, based on the information you've provided, you are not eligible for Child Benefit at this time. If Alex's circumstances change in the future (for example, if they enrol in approved education or training), you may wish to reassess your eligibility.

If you have any other questions, please don't hesitate to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old and is not currently in approved full-time education or approved training. To qualify for Child Benefit for a child aged 16-20, the child must be enrolled in approved education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or equivalent qualifications up to and including A-level or equivalent) or on an approved training course. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, aged 20, who is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those aged 16-20 who remain in qualifying education or training. As Alex does not meet these criteria, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
